title: Metformin vs Insulin Therapy in Type 2 Diabetes: A Systematic Review
abstract: This systematic review analyzed 15 randomized controlled trials comparing metformin and insulin therapy in type 2 diabetes patients. Results show that metformin reduces HbA1c levels by 1.2% on average compared to baseline. Insulin therapy achieved greater glycemic control with HbA1c reduction of 1.8%. However, metformin showed superior weight management outcomes with average weight loss of 2.3kg versus weight gain of 1.5kg with insulin. Hypoglycemic episodes were significantly lower with metformin (12% vs 34% of patients). The study concludes that metformin remains first-line therapy for most type 2 diabetes patients, while insulin is reserved for advanced cases.
